user_query:
I keep a simple CSV log of bird sightings during quiet walks on local golf courses, and I’d like a small event-style automation that reacts when I finish a new log. Please build a local command-line tool that, in a scan-once mode, treats input/sightings as the ‘inbox’ and does the following for any unprocessed *.csv file it finds:

1) Compute statistics for that file and write a machine-readable summary to out/summary/<basename>.summary.json (e.g., input/sightings/2026-04-15_sightings.csv -> out/summary/2026-04-15_sightings.summary.json). The JSON must include:
- file: the relative path of the processed CSV
- date_range: { start_iso, end_iso } derived from the earliest and latest timestamps in the file (ISO 8601)
- total_records: number of rows (excluding header)
- total_birds_counted: sum of the ‘count’ column across all rows
- unique_species_count: number of distinct species in the file
- species_counts: object mapping species -> total count across the file
- sightings_by_habitat: object mapping habitat -> record count
- by_course: object keyed by course_name with each value containing:
  { records, total_birds, unique_species_count, species_counts, sightings_by_habitat, priority_species_present (array of any priority species found) }

2) Draft one email message per course present in the CSV. Save each to out/emails/<basename>__<course-slug>.txt, where <course-slug> is the course name lowercased with spaces replaced by hyphens and non-alphanumerics removed. Each draft must begin with exactly these three header lines:
- To: <comma-separated recipients pulled from input/config/notify_config.json>
- From: <email_from from config>
- Subject: <subject_prefix from config> <course_name> <YYYY-MM-DD> to <YYYY-MM-DD>: <unique_species_count> species, <total_birds> birds
Then a blank line, followed by a short body that:
- lists the top 3 species by count with counts for that course,
- states total_records and sightings_by_habitat for that course,
- explicitly mentions any priority_species present for that course (or says "none").
Do not send any real email — only create the text drafts.

3) Maintain idempotence with a state file at state/processed.jsonl. Append one JSON line per processed CSV with keys: file (relative path), sha256 (hex digest of the file content), processed_at (ISO 8601 UTC), summary_path, email_paths (array). Re-running in scan-once mode must skip reprocessing a file if a line with the same sha256 already exists.

Use the configuration in input/config/notify_config.json. For this demo, run your tool once in scan-once mode so that it ingests the provided input/sightings/2026-04-15_sightings.csv and produces the summary and email drafts under out/ along with the updated state file. The tool can be implemented in any language, but it must be executable via a command-line program (for example: python scripts/sighting_watcher.py --scan-once --inbox input/sightings --config input/config/notify_config.json --out out --state state). All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
